name: Roll-Up with Extension
setup: Sitting upright, legs straight and together, feet dorsiflexed, toes pressing into Chair, hands holding handles, palms facing each other, trunk into C curve position
resistance: Socket T, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 7, Connector C, Spring Y/S

movement.action: Roll down, extend thoracic spine over Box
Roll up, return to start position

cues: Keep head aligned with spine
Maintain abdominal support
Place the Box at appropriate distance from Chair to maximize thoracic extension

name: Mini Roll-Up
setup: Lying supine on Box, facing Chair, head and shoulders lifted in Chest Lift position (Mat page 17), legs bent, feet in prehensile position, pressing into front of Chair, hands holding handles, palms facing each other
resistance: Socket T, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 7, Connector C, Spring Y/S

movement.action: Lower trunk, return to start position

cues: Maintain scapular stabilization
Maintain C curve
Focus on flexion of lumbar spine
Return to Chest Lift position after each repetition

name: Mini Roll-Up Oblique
setup: Starting in Mini Roll Up position (page 30), rotate trunk to right, Chest Lift with Rotation position (Mat page 18), left hand holding handle, right hand wrapped around left
resistance: Socket T, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 7, Connector C, Spring Y/S
cues: Keep weight evenly distributed on both feet
Keep pelvis stable
Maximize rotation while avoiding lateral flexion
Move head, shoulders and chest together as one unit
Keep nose aligned over sternum

name: Chest Expansion
setup: Sitting upright on Box, facing Chair, legs hip width apart, feet on floor between Chair and Box, arms straight and forward of trunk, hands holding handles, palms facing back
resistance: Socket T, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 3, Connector C, Spring Y/S

movement.action: Move arms forward, return to start position

cues: Maintain trunk stabilization keeping body upright
Maintain straight arms
Move arms 20 degrees forward and 20 degrees back of trunk

name: Biceps
setup: Starting in Chest Expansion position (page 32), arms straight and forward, in line with springs, palms facing up
resistance: Socket T, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 7, Connector C, Spring Y/S

movement.action: Straighten arms, return to start position

cues: Maintain trunk stabilization keeping body upright
Keep upper arms straight and forward in line with springs
Straighten elbows completely following each repetition
Maintain neutral position of wrists

name: Rhomboids 1
setup: Starting in Chest Expansion position (page 32), arms forward at shoulder height, palms facing down
resistance: Socket T, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 7, Connector C, Spring Y/S

movement.action: Straighten arms, return to start position

cues: Maintain trunk stabilization keeping body upright
Initiate movement by reaching elbows out to sides and back
Move arms along horizontal line at shoulder height

name: Rhomboids 2
setup: Starting in Chest Expansion position (page 32), arms forward at shoulder height, palms facing down
resistance: Socket T, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 7, Connector C, Spring Y/S

movement.action: Move elbows back, bending arms, glide scapulae together
Glide scapulae apart, move elbows forward, straightening arms, return to start position

cues: Maintain trunk stabilization keeping body upright
Move arms along horizontal line at shoulder height
Initiate by reaching elbows out to sides and back
Avoid scapular elevation when adducting scapulae

name: Hug-a-Tree
setup: Sitting upright, back against chair, legs hip width apart, feet on floor between Chair and Box, hands holding handles, arms in T position, micro-bend in elbows, palms facing forward
resistance: Socket T, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 7, Connector C, Spring Y/S

movement.action: Move arms back, return to start position

cues: Maintain scapular stabilization
Move arms along horizontal line at shoulder height
Move arms forward until parallel with each other
Keep hands in peripheral vision when opening arms

name: Salute
setup: Starting in Hug-a-Tree position (page 36), arms bent, hands holding handles, in line with eyes, fists facing forward
resistance: Socket T, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 7, Connector C, Spring Y/S

movement.action: Bend arms, return to start position

cues: Maintain scapular stabilization
Keep elbows lifted and reaching outward
Point fist or fingers in direction of movement

name: Hundred Prep
setup: Lying supine with head toward F2 Chair, feet on floor, heels pressing into the box, holding handles, arms straight, perpendicular, slight tension in springs
resistance: Socket T, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 3, Connector O, Spring Y/S

movement.action: Lift head, shoulders and chest, lower arms down to sides of body
Lift arms, to 90 degrees in shoulder joint, lower chest and head, return to start position

cues: Bias toward a posterior tilt of pelvis
Lift into spinal flexion as much as possible to reduce neck tension
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization

name: Leg Changes 1
resistance: Socket T, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 3, Connector O, Spring Y/S
cues: Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization
Maintain consistent angle of knee joint (90 degrees)
Maintain consistent height of chest lift

name: Frog
setup: Lying supine with head toward chair, neutral spine position, feet dorsiflexed in straps, knees bent in Frog position, hands holding levers, elbows and shoulders 90 degrees angle
resistance:
Socket T, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 1, Connector O, Spring Y/S
Resistance: Socket B, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 8, Connector O, Spring N/A

movement.action: Bend legs, return to start position

cues: Keep squeezing heels together
Avoid bending knees beyond 90 degrees

name: Circles Down
setup: Lying supine with head toward chair, neutral spine position, legs straight, perpendicular to floor, hips externally rotated, feet in straps plantarflexed, hands holding levers, elbows and shoulders 90 degrees angle
resistance:
Socket T, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 1, Connector O, Spring Y/S
Resistance: Socket B, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 8, Connector O, Spring N/A

movement.action: Open legs and circle around, lift legs, return to start position

cues: Focus on hip disassociation
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization
Draw straight line down center, squeezing heels together
Draw two semi-circles back to back as legs circle around

name: Circles Up
setup: Lying supine with head toward chair, neutral spine position, legs straight, perpendicular to floor, hips externally rotated, feet in straps plantarflexed, hands holding levers, elbows and shoulders 90 degrees angle
resistance:
Socket T, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 1, Connector O, Spring Y/S
Resistance: Socket B, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 8, Connector O, Spring N/A

movement.action: Open legs out to sides, circle legs around and together
Lift legs, return to start position

cues: Focus on hip disassociation
Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization
Draw two semi-circles back to back as legs circle around
Draw straight line up center, squeezing heels together

name: Openings
setup: Lying supine with head toward chair, neutral spine position, legs straight on a diagonal line (45 to 60 degrees), hips externally rotated, feet in straps, plantarflexed, hands holding levers, elbows and shoulders 90 degrees
resistance:
Socket T, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 1, Connector O, Spring Y/S
Resistance: Socket B, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 8, Connector O, Spring N/A

movement.action: Close legs, return to start position

cues: Maintain pelvic lumbar stabilization
Keep feet moving at a consistent height from floor as legs open and close
Move within the limits of pelvic lumbar control

name: Bottom Lift
setup: Lying supine on box facing F2 Chair, in neutral spine position, feet placed on chair, hip width apart, legs parallel, knees bent, shins parallel to floor, hands holding handles, arms straight, in line with shoulders, palms facing down
resistance: Socket T, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 7, Connector O, Spring Y/S
cues: Articulate through each vertebra as spine rolls up and down
Keep adductors engage and legs parallel
Avoid straightening legs and "pushing away"

name: Semi-Circle
setup: Lying supine on box facing F2 Chair, lower back extended over curve of box, pelvis resting between box and chair, legs parallel, knees bent, feet placed on chair, hip width apart, holding handles, palms facing down, arms in line with shoulder
resistance: Socket T, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 7, Connector O, Spring Y/S
cues: Articulate through each vertebra as spine rolls up and down
Keep adductors engage and legs parallel
Avoid straightening legs and "pushing away"

name: Lunge Kneeling
setup: Standing, front leg bent, knee over ankle, knee of other leg placed on chair, shin resting on chair, hands holding levers
resistance: Socket Box, Lever Orientation -90, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 1, Connector N/A, Spring N/A

movement.action: Stretch hip flexors of leg on chair (3-5 breaths)
Dorsiflex foot on floor, straighten leg (3-5 breaths)
Bend front leg, return to start position

cues: Bias toward posterior tilt of pelvis during hip flexor stretch
Bias toward anterior tilt of pelvis during hamstring stretch
During hamstring stretch try to flatten back and keep pelvis square

name: Swan
setup: Lying prone on top of F2 chair facing the back, legs straight and together, hips externally rotated, parallel to floor (as close as possible), hands holding handles, elbows out to sides
resistance: Socket T, Lever Orientation 0, Lever Length 1, Lever Direction 7, Connector N/A, Spring N/A

movement.action: Bend arms, lowering trunk return to start position

cues: Maintain abdominal engagement
Articulate spine into extension starting from crown of head
Keep legs straight and adducted
Maintain consistent height of legs throughout (as close to horizontal as possible)
